Q: A bird watcher records the number of birds he sees each day. On Monday, he sees 200 birds. He sees 20 less than double that many on Tuesday. On Wednesday, he sees 10 more than Monday. What is the total number of birds he sees on these three days?

A: On Tuesday, he sees 20 less than double that many birds on Monday, so on Tuesday he sees 2 * 200 - 20 = 380 birds. On Wednesday, he sees 10 more than Monday, so on Wednesday he sees 200 + 10 = 210 birds. The total number of birds he sees on these three days is 300 + 380 + 210 = 900 birds.

Q: The average of four consecutive even numbers is 15. What is the greatest number?
Options:
(A) 16
(B) 18
(C) 14
(D) 12
(E) 15
A: Four consecutive even numbers can be written as x, x + 2, x + 4, and x + 6. The average is x + x + 2 + x + 4 + x + 6 = 15 * 4. Simplifying, we get 4x + 12 = 60. So, 4x = 48 and x = 12. The greatest number of four consecutive even numbers is x + 6 = 12 + 6 = 18. So, the answer is (B) 1
